summary: Could not find useful documentation on bson_error_t
description: Function calls provide error results in bson_error_t but clicking on the link for details just goes to main documentation page.  Documentation of common error codes would be helpful.  I presume that most error codes are not driver specific, but are returned by the mongodb and documentation can be found there.  But, how is dropped network connection handled by the driver?

summary: Useful links from mongoc to libbson docs
description:
http://api.mongodb.com/c/current/tutorial.html#creating-bson-documents

"See the libbson documentation for all of the types that can be appended to a bson_t."

This really should be link to the libbson docs, and the bson_t docs.

I think there should also be a more prominent link from the main reference page to libbson. Its such a fundamental part of mongoc it should be linked better.

summary: Unexpected error using bson_writer and bson_append_binary
bson_new_from_buffer document is unfinished
description:
"Description" and "Returns" sections are "TODO":

http://api.mongodb.org/libbson/current/bson_new_from_buffer.html

summary: Eliminate copy of command reply document
description:
mongoc_client_command_simple takes a pointer to a bson_t, *into* which it must copy the command reply. In the past, users who wanted to avoid the copy could use mongoc_client_command instead. (It returns a cursor which holds the original RPC, avoiding the copy.)

But the new find / getMore commands required me to separate commands from cursors (CDRIVER-906) and send all "find" commands through the cursor-less command path, which copies the reply. (See _mongoc_cursor_find_command.)

Eliminate the copy:

1. Add uint8_t *bson_reserve_buffer(bson_t, size_t).
2. Use bson_reserve_buffer to preallocate the reply's internal buffer as soon as we know the reply's size.
3. Receive the server response directly into the reply buffer.

summary: Inconsistent error reporting when advancing completed OP_QUERY and command cursor
description:
While revising older PHPC tests to account for proper behavior noted in CDRIVER-1221, I realized another inconsistency between legacy and command cursor iteration.

{{_mongoc_cursor_next()}} and {{_mongoc_cursor_cursorid_next()}} are used for OP_QUERY and command cursor iteration, respectively. Both return true or false depending on whether a BSON document has been read (i.e. {{*bson}} is checked), although they differ in how {{cursor->done}} is calculated.

An superfluous call to {{_mongoc_cursor_next()}} results in a cursor error being set ("Cannot advance a completed or failed cursor."); however, a superfluous call to {{_mongoc_cursor_cursorid_next()}} raise no such error. In PHPC, this means that extra iteration on a complete cursor may or may not throw an exception, depending on the underlying protocol.

Is it feasible to make these two methods consistent, either by having {{_mongoc_cursor_cursorid_next()}} set an error or removing the exception error from {{_mongoc_cursor_next()}}?

summary: BCON_NEW segfault with 3 items in list
Enforce client certificate in evergreen
description:
Currently our mongo-orchestration config uses {{"sslWeakCertificateValidation": true}}, which means we never actually test if we provide client certificates properly to the server.

We should remove this option, and instead enable sslMode: requireSSL, which will make the server reject the connection if we don't provide it.

summary: ORACLE Database to MONGODB migration
description:
1.How to migrate multiple tables of oracle to mongodb ?

2.how to migrate index associated with table to mongodb?

3. how to migrated tables associated with parent-child to mongodb?

4. how to migrate partitioned tables to mongodbs?

5.how to migrate other objects (sequences,synonyms etc) of oracle to mongodb?

summary: libbson and libmongoc should compile with -std=c99 and -D_XOPEN_SOURCE=600
description:
libmongoc and libbson should compile with -std=c99 and -X_OPEN_SOURCE=600. 

There's plenty of room for things like _GNU_SOURCE for syscall() on linux, but we should be able to fall back to c99 and posix from 2004

summary: bson_ascii_strtoll "base" option
Copy strings referred by mongoc_ssl_opts_t
description:
As reported by [~acm] here:

  https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-cxx-driver/pull/490

... it's surprising and dangerous that mongoc_client_set_ssl_opts and mongoc_client_pool_set_ssl_opts copy only the options struct, and not also the strings to which it refers.

summary: Update Decimal128 implementation to be spec compliant
description:
There are handful of cases where we are supposed to error (per driver spec) but don't.
We need to update the test json with the missing tests and new ones when added.

summary: Test durations misformatted on Windows
Remove malloc for Decimal128 conversion during BSON->JSON
description:
Since bson_decimal128_from_string () requires a null terminated string, the  BSON to JSON conversion for bson_decimal128_t allocates a new string+1 since the input isn't null terminated.


We should be able to avoid that extra malloc, either though stack allocation or passing the length to the bson_decimal128_from_string () function.

summary: Deprecate mongoc_init_ctor/dtor
description:
We should deprecate the mongoc_init_ctor/dtor attribute constructor/destructor craziness.  It's gcc specific (so code that works on *nix will fail on other platforms) and prevents users from setting necessary callbacks before calling init (like the memory interface).

Hannes added MONGOC_NO_AUTOMATIC_GLOBALS option in 1.3.5.

In 1.4, shout a warning if automatic initialization and destruction are left turned on.

Remove automatic init / destruct in 2.0.

summary: Crash after Kerberos plugin cleanup runs twice
description:
{panel:title=Summary}
In some configurations, the following sequence leads to the MIT Kerberos5 library being destroyed twice during process exit:

# process starts, loads libmongoc, which registers its auto-destructor
# libmongoc loads Cyrus, which loads its Kerberos plugin, which registers the Kerberos plugin auto-destructor
# main() exits
# the Kerberos plugin's auto-destructor runs
# libmongoc's auto-destructor calls sasl_client_done, which closes the Kerberos plugin shared lib
# closing the Kerberos plugin shared lib triggers its auto-destructor *again*

The solution for C programs is to configure the driver with {{./configure  --enable-automatic-init-and-cleanup=no}} and ensure they call mongoc_init and mongoc_cleanup explicitly. In version 2.0, the driver will remove the automatic init and cleanup feature.
{panel}

*Original report:*

I found an assertion in kerberos originating in the C driver while testing the CXX driver master branch (commit 8dc0b5b).  Backtrace follows:

{noformat}
$ lldb ./src/mongocxx/test/test_driver

===============================================================================
All tests passed (903 assertions in 60 test cases)

Assertion failed: (r == 0), function k5_mutex_lock, file ../../include/k5-thread.h, line 384.
Process 47444 stopped
* thread #1: tid = 0x9686ee, 0x00007fff97fdaf06 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 10, queue = 'com.apple.main
-thread', stop reason = signal SIGABRT
    frame #0: 0x00007fff97fdaf06 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 10

    0x7fff97fdaf10 <+20>: retq
(lldb) bt
* thread #1: tid = 0x9686ee, 0x00007fff97fdaf06 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 10, queue = 'com.apple.main
-thread', stop reason = signal SIGABRT
  * frame #0: 0x00007fff97fdaf06 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 10
    frame #1: 0x00007fff922b94ec libsystem_pthread.dylib`pthread_kill + 90

During libmongoc compilation, I did notice numerous warnings of the type: {{clang: warning: argument unused during compilation: '-pthread'}}

There's [this warning|https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E19120-01/open.solaris/819-2145/sasl.intro-52/index.html] that libraries should not call {{sasl_done}}:

bq. Libraries should not call sasl_done(). Applications should exercise caution when calling sasl_done() to avoid interference with any libraries that might be using libsasl.

summary: Allow for more finegrained SSL connection control regarding invalid hostnames and invalid certificate flags
description:
The mongo client has two options that deal with invalid/incorrect certificates:

{code}
  --sslAllowInvalidHostnames         allow connections to servers with 
                                     non-matching hostnames
  --sslAllowInvalidCertificates      allow connections to servers with invalid 
                                     certificates
{code}

But the C driver only has a flag in ssl_opts to turn off invalid certificate checking (weak_cert_validation): http://api.mongodb.org/c/current/mongoc_ssl_opt_t.html

PHP's SSL layer has something similar through its ``verify_peer_name`` and ``allow_self_signed`` SSL context options.

This currently means, that by using the CDRIVER in Hippo, I can't make all tests pass, as the peer name ("server") does not match the server name as in CDRIVER-841. I can turn on ``allow_self_signed`` to allow connecting, but that's more than I should be having to do. Allowing an extra option specifically for peer verification would be required to allow Hippo to past the Phongo tests.

summary: Support GridFS custom file_id methods
description:
In accordance with the [GridFS Spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst], implement mongoc_gridfs_file_set_id (mongoc_gridfs_file_t *file, const bson_value_t *id) so users can specify id's of any type for files. 

The C Driver does not implement the following methods:
open_upload_stream_with_id 
upload_from_stream_with_id

summary: mongoc_collection_create_index should return index document
description:
The legacy driver would return the inserted index as a bson document. mongoc_collection_create_index should exhibit the same behavior.

This will require an enhanced new function, mongoc_collection_create_index_2 or something, that returns additional info.

History: we created mongoc_collection_create_index_2, renamed to mongoc_collection_create_index_with_write_concern, and now we're renaming it mongoc_collection_create_index_with_opts. All of this is for 1.5.0.

summary: Return type of mongoc_client_command_simple_with_server_id should be bool in documentation
description: Change the return type of mongoc_client_command_simple_with_server_id in the documentation to reflect the return type in src/mongoc/mongoc-client.c

summary: Add instructions to CONTRIBUTING about add error codes / domains
Read preference should be validated before server selection
description:
From [this comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/211#discussion_r27255397] on the PR for CDRIVER-590:

bq. Previously, such a URI would result in the client's read preference having tags with the primary mode, which is invalid according to the read preference spec and {{mongoc_read_prefs_is_valid()}}. That said, {{mongoc_read_prefs_is_valid()}} was never actually called anywhere, so I'm not sure how libmongoc handled such a structure.

So, although {{mongoc_read_prefs_is_valid()}} exists, it's never called anywhere (apart from the URI validation added in CDRIVER-590). If we compare this to write concerns, those are checked before any write operation and can yield an error before we send anything to the server. I expect any read operation using a read preference should do the same and yield an error before attempting server selection.

summary: Move or copy TLS instructions to mongoc_advanced_connections.page
description: Seems weird that the code examples for TLS connections are in doc for X509 authentication. Most TLS users are merely encrypting their transport, not *also* using TLS for authentication.

description:
See https://ci.appveyor.com/project/markbenvenuto/mongo-cxx-driver/build/3.0.0.182#L2004

summary: Some server selection tests are skipped
description: The JSON test runner intends to recurse into subdirectories like "json/server_selection/ReplicaSetNoPrimary/read", but doesn't properly assemble paths to subdirectories.

summary: mongoc_collection_find_and_modify_with_opts can return true on writeConcernError
description: This function should return false if there's a writeConcernError.

summary: SecondaryPreferred fails if primary matches tags but secondaries don't.
description:
Server Selection logic bug. Assume a primary P and secondary S, and a read preference with mode SecondaryPreferred and a tag set. S doesn't match the tag set, so the driver should fall back to using the primary.

There's a bug if P happens to match the read preference's tag set. The driver selects *no* servers instead of using the primary.

mongoc_topology_description_suitable_servers's logic is:

1. Set has_secondary true, because there is a secondary S.
2. Select "eligible" servers according to the tag set: this leaves only P.
3. Since there is at least one eligible server, *leave* has_secondary true.
4. Since has_secondary is true and mode is SecondaryPreferred, remove P from suitable servers.
5. Now there are no suitable servers, selection fails.

The bug is near #3: has_secondary should only be true if there is an eligible *secondary*, rather than if there is any eligible server?

Another option is to partly rewrite the C Driver's server selection based on how other clients do SecondaryPreferred: try selection with mode Secondary, if that fails then select with mode Primary. For PrimaryPreferred, vice versa.

summary: Avoid realloc in bulk execution
description: {{_mongoc_write_command}} splits a stream of documents (all inserts, all updates, or all deletes) to the server 16 mb at a time. Between each 16 mb batch it destroys the command document {{cmd}} and reinitializes it. This wastes time in {{malloc}}. Just {{bson_reinit}} it after the first batch, and bson_destroy only at the end.

summary: mongoc_client_new function does not return NULL when it fail to connect with server
mongoc_collection_validate doesn't always init "reply"
description:
The documentation for mongoc_collection_validate says:

reply [an argument] is always initialized and must be destroyed with bson_destroy().

(see https://api.mongodb.com/c/current/mongoc_collection_validate.html)


The implementation doesn't always initialize reply. In particular, if the options passed are invalid, then reply is left alone, which means if a client tries to bson_destroy() it, they might end up accessing uninitialized memory.

I think this is like a one-line fix, but there might be bugs just like this one all over

summary: libbson serializes $binary to JSON in the wrong order
Accept uppercase hex characters in bson_oid_is_valid()
description:
[{{bson_oid_is_valid()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/libbson/blob/1.3.5/src/bson/bson-oid.c#L456] requires lowercase characters, but [{{bson_oid_parse_hex_char()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/libbson/blob/1.3.5/src/bson/bson-oid.h#L146] (used by [{{bson_oid_init_from_string_unsafe}}|https://github.com/mongodb/libbson/blob/1.3.5/src/bson/bson-oid.h#L204]) is case-insensitive.

{{bson_oid_is_valid()}} is documented as "return true if @str can be passed to bson_oid_init_from_string()." Allowing it to accept uppercase characters satisfies this contract and would remove the need for calling code to convert their input strings to lowercase.

summary: Allow compiling against OpenSSL 1.1.X
Set SSL_OP_NO_COMPRESSION by default
description:
TLS Compression is dangerous and should be avoided on the public internet. It is disabled by default in OpenSSL 1.1.0 and later, but needs to be explicitly disabled for earlier releases.

From client perspective, we should protect our clients by ensuring SSL_OP_NO_COMPRESSION is set on the client side, making it irrelevant if the server lib supports it or not.

We should check if Secure Transport or Secure Channel needs similar workaround to disable compression

summary: Windows + OpenSSL Build variant in Evergreen
description: We need a build variant on Evergreen that compiles the driver on Windows against its OpenSSL for legacy support

summary: Issue a warning when certificate isn't found
description:
mongoc_ssl_extract_subject() should issue a warning when the filename doesn't exist, or isn't a certificate.

Our Darwin TLS implementation already does that, while OpenSSL doesn't.

summary: Don't verify certificate hostname for Unix Domain Sockets
description:
When connecting over UDS, doing certificate hostname verification makes no sense.

We should detect this a little earlier and set allow_invalid_hostname=true automatically so individual TLS implementation doesn't have to do silly checks for it like https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/master/src/mongoc/mongoc-stream-tls-secure-transport.c#L401

summary: BSON_EXTRA_ALIGN 0 does not compile with MSVC
description:
If BSON_EXTRA_ALIGN is 0 the BSON_ALIGNED_BEGIN macro defined [here|https://github.com/mongodb/libbson/blob/33851d13ff3a392/src/bson/bson-macros.h#L127] is invalid.

MSVC does not allow mathematical expressions in align:
{quote}
__declspec( align( # ) ) declarator

...

\# is the alignment value. *Valid entries are integer powers of two from 1 to 8192 (bytes), such as 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, or 64.*
{quote}
https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/83ythb65.aspx

The cmake file always sets BSON_EXTRA_ALIGN to 1 so this was never noticed. 
https://github.com/mongodb/libbson/blob/33851d13/CMakeLists.txt#L70-L92

summary: Improve SSL connection error messages
scan-build warnings (libbson)

summary: Secure Transport subject reversed
description:
It looks like the manual RFC2253 creation in {{Secure Transport}} does it in reverse order compared to OpenSSL.

We should match the OpenSSL order as that is what we originally did.

summary: ASAN/UBSAN: misaligned address
description:
CRAN reports some misaligned address errors for mongolite:

 - [clang-ubsan|http://www.stats.ox.ac.uk/pub/bdr/memtests/clang-UBSAN/mongolite-Ex.Rout]
 - [gcc-asan|http://www.stats.ox.ac.uk/pub/bdr/memtests/gcc-ASAN/mongolite/]
 - [gss-ubsan|http://www.stats.ox.ac.uk/pub/bdr/memtests/gcc-UBSAN/mongolite-Ex.Rout]

I did compile the with {code}-DBSON_EXTRA_ALIGN{code}. Is there something I can do to fix this?

summary: Test with multiple mongoses
description:
orchestration_configs/sharded_clusters/auth.json starts two mongoses but we only connect to the one on 27017. There should be an *additional* Evergreen variant that connects the driver to both.

The work will happen in and around .mci.yml and test_framework_get_uri_str_no_auth

summary: Accept NULL endptr in bson_ascii_strtoll()
description: [strtoll(3)|http://linux.die.net/man/3/strtoll] allows {{NULL}} for its second argument, making ({{endptr}} assignment optional. I propose allowing {{bson_ascii_strtoll()}} to do the same for consistency.

summary: Error code for connection timeouts
description: When a connection times out, the error domain is set to MONGOC_ERROR_STREAM when it should rather be MONGOC_ERROR_CLIENT.

summary: Man pages installation broken
description:
$ ./configure --enable-man-pages
...
$ make
...
$ make install DESTDIR=...
...

 /usr/bin/install -c -m 644 doc/man/installing.3 doc/man/version.3 doc/man/installing.3 doc/man/version.3 '/dev/shm/BUILDROOT/libbson-1.3.1-1.fc23.remi.x86_64/usr/share/man/man3'

/usr/bin/install: will not overwrite just-created '/dev/shm/BUILDROOT/libbson-1.3.1-1.fc23.remi.x86_64/usr/share/man/man3/version.3' with 'doc/man/version.3'
Makefile:1846: recipe for target 'install-man3' failed

Trying to install twice each files fails.

summary: Bugs in single-threaded selection timeout
description:
Two bugs with mongoc_topology_select in 1.3.x when serverSelectionTryOnce is turned off.

Bug #1: instead of sleeping a half second between server checks, mongoc_topology_select sleeps half a second *longer* between each server check until the next sleep would exceed the server selection timeout.

Consider the 1.3.5 code in mongoc_topology_select. "try_once" is false, connectTimeoutMS is 500, socketTimeoutMS is 60,000:

{code}
/* call the start time "0" */
loop_start = loop_end = 0
expire_at = 30 seconds
next_update = something negative
topology->stale = true

first loop iteration:
scan_ready = something negative
sleep_usec = something negative
spend, let's say, 5 ms in _mongoc_topology_do_blocking_scan
topology->last_scan = now = 5ms

second loop iteration:
scan_ready = 505ms
sleep_usec = 505ms
sleep for 505ms
spend 5ms in _mongoc_topology_do_blocking_scan
topology->last_scan = now = 510ms

third loop iteration:
scan_ready = 1010ms
sleep_usec = 1010ms
sleep for 1010ms
spend 5ms in _mongoc_topology_do_blocking_scan
{code}

Bug #2: when scan_ready has advanced enough to exceed expire_at, the error message should be the standard "serverselectiontimeoutms timed out", not "minheartbeatfrequencyms not reached yet".

summary: Include mongoc-trace.h from mongoc.h so other drivers don't need to include it separately
